T1566.name: Phishing
T1566.description: Adversaries may send phishing messages to gain access to victim systems.

T1566.001.name: Spearphishing Attachment
T1566.001.description: Adversaries may send spearphishing emails with a malicious attachment.

T1566.002.name: Spearphishing Link
T1566.002.description: Adversaries may send spearphishing emails with a malicious link.

T1059.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter
T1059.description: Adversaries may abuse command and script interpreters to execute commands.

T1059.001.name: PowerShell
T1059.001.description: Adversaries may abuse PowerShell commands and scripts for execution.

T1071.name: Application Layer Protocol
T1071.description: Adversaries may communicate using application layer protocols to avoid detection.

T1071.001.name: Web Protocols
T1071.001.description: Adversaries may communicate using web protocols (HTTP/HTTPS) for C2.

T1110.name: Brute Force
T1110.description: Adversaries may use brute force techniques to attempt access to accounts.

T1110.001.name: Password Guessing
T1110.001.description: Adversaries may guess passwords to attempt access to accounts.

T1204.name: User Execution
T1204.description: An adversary may rely upon specific actions by a user to gain execution.

T1486.name: Data Encrypted for Impact
T1486.description: Adversaries may encrypt data on target systems to interrupt availability.

T1048.name: Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol
T1048.description: Adversaries may steal data by exfiltrating it over a different protocol.

T1105.name: Ingress Tool Transfer
T1105.description: Adversaries may transfer tools or files from an external system into a compromised environment.

T1078.name: Valid Accounts
T1078.description: Adversaries may obtain and abuse credentials of existing accounts.

T1053.name: Scheduled Task/Job
T1053.description: Adversaries may abuse task scheduling to execute malicious code.
